title: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
slides: Can a no-code platform beat big AI labs at their own game?
Base44 just launched its own proprietary model.
Most vibe coding tools rely on third-party APIs.
Now your app's logic stays entirely on Base44's infrastructure.
Check if your current stack locks you into someone else's model.
thread_text:
1. Big AI labs are obsolete?

Base44 built a proprietary model while most tools rely on third-party APIs. Your app's logic stays entirely on their infrastructure.

2. No-code meets infrastructure.

Unlike vibe coding platforms that outsource intelligence, Base44 keeps everything in-house. That changes how you deploy logic.

3. Lock-in is the real cost.

Most tools today tie you to someone else's model. Base44 lets you own the entire stack from prompt to inference.

4. Proprietary model, zero switching.

You don't need to swap APIs when pricing changes. Base44's model is built into its own platform from day one.

5. Check your current stack.

Ask yourself: if your model provider raised prices tomorrow, could you switch? If not, test Base44's model this week.
video_script: Your app logic stays on Base44.
Is your stack locked into someone?

script: Your app logic stays on Base44.
Is your stack locked into someone?

instagram.caption:
Most no-code AI tools let you build apps but outsource the actual intelligence to third-party models. 🧠

Base44 just launched its own proprietary model, keeping your app’s logic entirely on its own infrastructure—no reliance on external APIs that can change pricing or policies overnight.

For anyone creating content or tools with AI, this means your workflow isn’t locked into someone else’s stack. You own the system from prompt to inference.

Is your current setup a rental, or do you actually control the model underneath? 🔑
